aliases: ["Rainwater harvesting (RWH)","Soil and water conservation (SWC)","In-situ rainwater harvesting","Ex-situ rainwater harvesting","Check dams","Farm ponds","Percolation tanks","Contour bunding","Terracing","Gully plugs","Earthen dams","Micro-catchments","Hafirs","Negarim microcatchments","Vallerani technique"]
description_short: Deliberate capture, storage, and managed use of surface runoff and rainwater through structural and land-based interventions to improve agricultural water availability, recharge groundwater, and reduce erosion and flood risk.
description_technical: Water harvesting and conservation encompasses interventions that intercept, retain, and store precipitation and surface runoff for productive use or groundwater recharge. Structural sub-practices include check dams, farm ponds, percolation tanks, gully plugs, earthen and small dams, and cisterns. Land-based sub-practices include contour bunding, terracing, tied ridging, contour trenching, and micro-catchment systems. Site selection is determined primarily by slope, drainage morphology, runoff potential (SCS Curve Number), soil texture, and rainfall. The dominant spatial method across 85 stocktake papers is GIS-based multi-criteria evaluation (MCE) using AHP-weighted overlay validated against DEM-derived hydrological parameters.
upfront_cost_usd_ha: {"low":50,"high":800,"currency":"USD","source_note":"Low end: simple contour bunds at community labour cost (~$50/ha). High end: engineered check dams or lined farm ponds (~$800/ha). Ter\u00e9ncio et al. (2017) calculate EUR/m3 storage cost ratios. Ibrahim et al. (2019) apply cost-minimisation in dam site selection. Ahmed et al. (2023) monetise ecosystem-service benefits in USD."}
